worldview: Residual headroom on the fixed pr1394 SP8192/GPTQ/recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip base is more likely to come from correcting train-to-deploy contract mismatches and abrupt regime changes than from scalar retunes. This trajectory therefore favors bounded program families that alter recurrence activation, export-state choice, or deployment representation under the real quantized_sliding_window objective.

hypotheses.name: Staged Recurrence Activation
hypotheses.mechanism: Replace the single enable_looping_at step-function with a staged depth-recurrence program that introduces shared loop repeats progressively, so the model does not absorb the recurrence burden as one abrupt phase change.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: The best way to use parameter-shared depth recurrence is a single abrupt off-to-on switch at one training fraction.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: The family changes the training program from one fixed topology transition to a multi-stage topology schedule. The exact fractions are secondary; the first-order claim is that recurrence should be introduced as a staged regime, not a one-time event.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Use the catalog loop_ramp family on the fixed pr1394 base and compare abrupt activation controls versus a 0->1->2 repeat ramp with one seed plus two controls.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Before final eval, the ramped run should show a smoother loss trajectory around the recurrence turn-on window and end with lower pre-quant val_bpb that carries through to quantized_sliding_window.
hypotheses.falsifier: If the ramped run does not beat both controls on score_bpb and shows no improvement around the recurrence activation window relative to the abrupt switch, the staged-transition story is false for this base.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: Distinct from optimizer retunes and selector lines because it changes when the effective model depth exists during training. It tests the recurrence mechanism itself rather than coefficients around it.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Kept because it breaks a real false invariant about recurrence activation, has a crisp executable probe in the current catalog, and is judged on the downstream lane it plausibly affects.

hypotheses.name: Multi-EMA Export Selector
hypotheses.mechanism: Maintain multiple candidate late states (raw, base EMA, fast EMA, slow EMA) and select the winning artifact instead of assuming one fixed EMA decay is the right export state for this base.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: A single fixed EMA decay is always the correct export-state contract for the pr1394 stack.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This is not 'tune EMA decay'. It changes the artifact-selection contract from one precommitted averaging path to a bounded selector over multiple late-state candidates.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Use the catalog ema_selector family and choose among raw, EMA, fast EMA, and slow EMA by final pre-quant val_bpb on the same training run.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Candidate states should separate late in training, and the selected branch should improve downstream score_bpb over the default EMA export even when the training path is otherwise unchanged.
hypotheses.falsifier: If all candidate states cluster within control spread and the chosen branch does not improve quantized_sliding_window, then export-state choice is not the bottleneck here.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This is the clearest anti-dominant lane in the pack: it attacks artifact choice rather than training dynamics. It is distinct from quantization families because the training run stays fixed while only the export-state chooser changes.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Kept because the mechanism is first-order, the probe is especially clean on a fixed base, and the lane matches the deployment-facing metric contract.

hypotheses.name: Late Quantization-Envelope Internalization
hypotheses.mechanism: During late training, gradually project weights toward the row-wise clip envelope implied by the final GPTQ export, so the deployed model is no longer forced to absorb the full train-to-quantization mismatch only at serialization time.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: End-of-run GPTQ plus SDClip can absorb deployment distortion without any training-time pressure toward the eventual clip envelope.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: The core move is to internalize a deployment constraint into training. The anneal parameters are just one realization of a broader family where late training is explicitly shaped by the export path.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Use the catalog quant_clip_anneal family with late-only clip-envelope pressure and compare the gap between pre-quant val_bpb and quantized_sliding_window against controls.
hypotheses.expected_observable: The pre-quant score may move modestly, but the stronger signature is a reduced degradation or improved translation into the final quantized_sliding_window score.
hypotheses.falsifier: If the train-to-deploy gap does not shrink, or if downstream score_bpb worsens despite comparable pre-quant behavior, then deployment-envelope internalization is not helping this stack.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: Distinct from selector search because it changes the training objective path, and distinct from sensitivity_bits because it shapes the weight distribution before export rather than reallocating bits at export.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Kept because it directly targets the primary metric's deployment bottleneck with an honest executable family, even though the exact late-training signal is somewhat noisier than selector probes.

hypotheses.name: Sensitivity-Allocated Matrix Bit Budget
hypotheses.mechanism: Replace uniform int6 matrix quantization with a bounded bit-allocation program that promotes the most Hessian-sensitive matrices and demotes the least sensitive ones while staying inside the same artifact budget class.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: Uniform matrix bitwidth is close enough to optimal that per-matrix sensitivity should not matter on the published pr1394 base.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This is not a bitwidth nudge. It changes the deployment representation contract from homogeneous precision to sensitivity-conditioned heterogeneous precision under a fixed budget.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Use the catalog sensitivity_bits family to promote top-sensitive matrices to int7 and demote low-sensitive matrices to int5 while keeping the rest at int6.
hypotheses.expected_observable: The winning signature is improved quantized_sliding_window score at essentially unchanged bytes_total, with recurring sensitivity winners rather than random matrix assignments.
hypotheses.falsifier: If mixed allocation does not beat uniform int6 at similar artifact size, or if the chosen high/low sensitivity sets do not show directional consistency, the representation-allocation story is false.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: Distinct from SDClip or GPTQ tuning because it changes where precision lives, not how one global quantizer is parameterized. It is also orthogonal to train-time recurrence and selector stories.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Kept because it changes a first-order deployment contract, is directly aligned with the downstream objective, and already exists as an honest executable family in the current catalog.

rejected_lines: skip_gate_schedule: executable, but the current realization is too close to a single-bias anneal around an already-active mechanism; for round-0 ontology search it risks collapsing into a gating retune rather than a new first-order family.

phase_split_optim: consequential in principle, but the available implementation is mostly LR and weight-decay multipliers around loop activation, so it reads as within-family exploitation before this harness has established which larger mechanism class is real.
